query: War and Peace Leo Tolstoy commonly cited English translation page count Pevear Volokhonsky pages

results.title: War and Peace (1869), by Leo Tolstoy, translated by Richard Pevear & Larissa Volokhonsky | ANZ LitLovers LitBlog
results.snippet: 300px-napoleons_retreat_from_moscow This was my third attempt to read *War and Peace,* and now I don’t understand why I found it difficult before. Tolstoy lionises Kutuzov a bit, and says he is the real hero of this war (p1085) but Russians treated him with contempt because he ‘failed’ to ‘defeat’ Napoleon – and yet he did exactly what the Russian people wanted him to do, that is, evict the invader with minimal loss of life. (p1087)  The tragedy of the Napoleonic retreat was that the Russians – pursuing only to ensure that the French did leave – also died in great numbers. On p683 he gives the reasons for the French defeat: they marched on Moscow without making preparation for the Russian winter, and they did not understand how hatred of the French led to the firing of towns such as Smolensk en route. 800px-moscow2c_st__basil27s_as_seen_from_the_westp1032-3 History claims that battles decide the fate of nations and that defeat equals conquest, but Tolstoy says that did not happen with Napoleon’s defeat of Moscow because the Russians didn’t play by the ‘rules’.
